narrative_focus: formalization + executable Python reference implementation + honest measured/projection split

scripts_used.purpose: Run the executable Python reference implementation on the 14-scenario suite and generated 10/30/50/100-service topologies.
Reproduce the model-based deep-topology projection study.
scripts_used.caveat: This script uses _simulate_phase_times and does not execute the verifier end-to-end.

scripts_used.purpose: Check whether the Rust workspace currently builds.

scripts_used.summary: Compilation/test mismatches across crates (e.g. cascade-types, cascade-bmc, cascade-graph, cascade-maxsat).

scripts_used.purpose: Exercise the prototype CLI on a checked-in example.
scripts_used.summary: Panicked with "Mismatch between definition and access of verbose".

scripts_used.purpose: Rebuild the paper PDF after narrative recovery.
scripts_used.summary: tool_paper.pdf rebuilt successfully with pdflatex run twice on 2026-03-17.

claims.statement: Bounded cascade reachability in the RTIG model is NP-complete.
claims.evidence: Proposition 1 in tool_paper.tex states NP-completeness, with membership by polynomial witness checking and NP-hardness via reduction.

claims.statement: CB-free RTIGs are monotone, enabling antichain pruning and a finite completeness bound for bounded model checking.
claims.evidence: Theorem 2 and Corollary 1 in tool_paper.tex establish monotonicity and the d*=diameter(G)×max_retries completeness bound.

claims.statement: The executable Python reference implementation agrees with the labels of all 14 hand-authored scenarios in benchmarks/empirical_evaluation.py.
claims.evidence: Rerun on 2026-03-17 produced TP=9, TN=5, FP=0, FN=0, i.e. 14/14 scenario-label agreement; average verification time 1.8ms; average repair time 3.6ms.
claims.caveat: These are transparent hand-authored scenarios, not an external audited benchmark corpus.

claims.statement: In that same 14-scenario rerun, the repair loop re-checks as sound on 13 of the 14 cascade scenarios.
claims.evidence: benchmarks/empirical_results.json reports repair_verified_sound=true for 13 scenarios and false for mesh_20_services; aggregate soundness in the rerun output is 92.9%.

claims.statement: Measured synthetic scaling of the Python reference implementation reaches 100-service generated graphs, with total runtime 1365.8ms (chain), 491.7ms (tree), and 3561.6ms (mesh).
claims.evidence: Rerun of benchmarks/empirical_evaluation.py reproduced the 10/30/50/100-service scaling table in benchmarks/empirical_results.json.
claims.caveat: These are generated graphs evaluated by the Python reference implementation, not end-to-end Rust CLI runs on real deployment corpora.

claims.statement: deep_cascade_results.json is a model-based projection study, not measured verifier runtime.
claims.evidence: benchmarks/deep_cascade_profiler.py computes costs through _simulate_phase_times() and writes them to deep_cascade_results.json; rerun reproduced those projections.

claims.statement: The Rust workspace is substantial engineering scaffolding but is not yet the core validated artifact: it builds, while tests and one checked example invocation currently fail.
claims.evidence: A 2026-03-17 line count over implementation/crates/**/*.rs yielded 79 files and 62,179 lines; cargo build passed; cargo test failed; a checked example CLI invocation panicked.
